stem: A agriculture study measures the yield per hectare (tonnes) for a sample of 15 farms. The sample mean is 34.3 tonnes with a sample standard deviation of 9.2 tonnes. However, 2 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 13 observations have a mean of 33.3 tonnes and standard deviation of 5.7 tonnes. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 34.3, 95% CI = [29.2, 39.4]. Without outliers: mean = 33.3, 95% CI = [30.1, 36.5]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [29.2, 39.4]. Point estimate: 33.8 tonnes.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [29.2, 39.4].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
